title: Multi-Agent System Checklist
concept: A review guide: roles specialized, formats structured, deadlock protection active, context pruned.

exercises.prompt: What did we use to see how different agents worked together in the last lesson?

options.text: We traced a task across agents
We focused on a single agent

exercises.explain: In the last lesson, we learned how to trace a task across different agents, helping us see how they work together.

cards.title: Multi-Agent System Checklist
cards.body: A multi-agent system uses different agents to complete tasks. Each agent has a specific role.

cards.title: What Are Specialized Roles?
cards.body: In multi-agent systems, each agent has a unique purpose, like planning, coding, or reviewing. This allows them to work efficiently!

cards.title: Agents as Team Members
cards.body: Think of agents like players in a soccer team. Each player has a specific position, like forward or defender, making the team stronger.

cards.title: Roles in Action
cards.body: In our last task, we had planning agents to create designs, coding agents to build them, and review agents to ensure everything worked well.

cards.title: Myth: All Agents Do Everything
cards.body: Many think one agent can do all tasks, but in reality, specialized roles make the system more effective.

cards.title: Multi-Agent System Elements
cards.body: Remember these key elements of multi-agent systems!

left.text: Each agent has a unique function.

exercises.prompt: What does having structured formats mean in a multi-agent system?
exercises.explain: Having structured formats means tasks are designed to be completed in a specific order, which helps agents collaborate better.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Deadlock protection prevents agents from getting stuck.
exercises.explain: True! Deadlock protection helps ensure that agents can continue to work without getting locked out from performing their tasks.

exercises.prompt: In multi-agent systems, ensuring __________ protection keeps the agents from getting stuck.
exercises.sentence: In multi-agent systems, ensuring deadlock protection keeps the agents from getting stuck.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'deadlock', as it refers specifically to the protection that prevents agents from being unable to proceed.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is NOT a part of the multi-agent checklist?
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'unstructured chaos'. A multi-agent system aims for structure and organization to be effective.

exercises.prompt: Which elements should you check in a multi-agent system?

options.text: Roles specialized, formats unstructured, deadlock protection active, context ignored
Roles specialized, formats structured, deadlock protection active, context pruned
Roles unassigned, formats chaotic, deadlock avoidant, context expanded
Roles flexible, formats solid, deadlock active, context clear

exercises.explain: The correct answer emphasizes having specialized roles, structured formats, active deadlock protection, and pruned context. This ensures efficient collaboration among agents.
